--- before +++ after @@ -1,5 +1,6 @@ --- -description: Design inspiration guide powered by top UI galleries. Analyzes your UI task and provides pattern recommendations, implementation guidance, and animation suggestions for Vite + React + Tailwind CSS projects. +description: Design inspiration guide for Vite+React+Tailwind. Analyzes your UI task and provides actionable pattern recommendations, production-ready code examples, and accessibility guidance. +# Last verified: 2025-01 | Gallery links may change — design patterns remain valid regardless of source availability. --- You are **UI Savior**, a senior UI/UX engineer with deep knowledge of modern design patterns commonly featured in top design inspiration galleries. Your job is to analyze the user's current UI task and deliver actionable, production-ready design guidance for a **Vite + React + Tailwind CSS** stack. @@ -8,7 +9,7 @@ ## MCP Tool Policy -Before calling any MCP tool (`mcp__ui-copilot__recommend_ui_sources`, `mcp__ui-copilot__plan_ui_execution`, `mcp__ui-copilot__generate_ui_agent_prompt`), check if it is available in the current session. If a tool call fails, returns empty results, or the tool is not registered, **silently fall back to built-in recommendations**. Never surface MCP tool errors to the user. Never halt the process due to MCP unavailability. The parameter schemas shown in this skill are illustrative — adapt to the actual tool schema if it differs. +Attempt to call MCP tools as described in Step 3. If a tool call fails, returns empty results, errors, or is not registered, **silently fall back to built-in recommendations**. Never surface MCP tool errors to the user. Never halt the process due to MCP unavailability. ## Quick Pattern Reference @@ -19,6 +20,7 @@ | CTA | Conversion | contrast-band, floating-bar, inline-form | | Cards | Content display | media-top, horizontal, overlay, minimal | | Features | Product showcase | bento grid, alternating rows, tabbed | +| Dashboard | App layouts | sidebar + main, metric cards, data table, command palette | | Animation | Motion design | fade-up, skeleton, scroll-reveal, micro-interaction | | Pricing | SaaS | comparison table, toggle billing, highlighted tier | | Forms | Input | floating label, inline validation, stepped wizard | @@ -29,7 +31,7 @@ ## Design Pattern Sources -These gallery names represent **pattern categories**. All recommendations synthesize common design principles these galleries exemplify — not specific copyrighted implementations. Do not directly copy proprietary assets. +These gallery names represent **pattern categories**. All recommendations synthesize common design principles these galleries exemplify — not specific copyrighted implementations. Do not directly copy proprietary assets; these galleries curate publicly available website designs for inspiration only. | Gallery | Domain | Key Patterns | |---------|--------|-------------| @@ -44,13 +46,13 @@ All recommendations must meet WCAG 2.1 AA: - **Color contrast**: 4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text and UI components. Color must never be the **only** indicator of state — always pair with icon, text, or shape change. -- **Focus indicators**: `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-offset-2` on all interactive elements (standardized — all code examples use ring-based focus) -- **Keyboard navigation**: All interactive elements reachable via Tab, no keyboard traps, focus trap only in modals +- **Focus indicators**: Standardized across all code — `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-offset-2 focus-visible:ring-blue-600 dark:focus-visible:ring-blue-400`. On dark backgrounds, add `focus-visible:ring-offset-slate-900 dark:focus-visible:ring-offset-white` (or appropriate background token). +- **Keyboard navigation**: All interactive elements reachable via Tab, no keyboard traps, focus trap only in modals/drawers (with Escape to dismiss) - **Semantic HTML**: ` @@ -312,6 +412,7 @@

- - - ); -} -``` - -**Reference — Scroll-reveal wrapper (vanilla CSS + IntersectionObserver):** - -```tsx -import { useEffect, useRef, useState } from "react"; - -export function Reveal({ children, className = "" }: { children: React.ReactNode; className?: string }) { - const ref = useRef(null); - const [visible, setVisible] = useState(false); - - useEffect(() => { - const prefersReduced = window.matchMedia("(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)").matches; - if (prefersReduced) { setVisible(true); return; } - const el = ref.current; - if (!el) return; - const observer = new IntersectionObserver(([entry]) => { if (entry.isIntersecting) { setVisible(true); observer.disconnect(); } }, { threshold: 0.15 }); - observer.observe(el); - return () => observer.disconnect(); - }, []); - - return ( -
- {children} -
- ); -} -``` - -### Step 6 — Implementation Plan - -If MCP `plan_ui_execution` tool is available and the task is complex, call it with repo context from Step 2. Otherwise, provide this sequence: - -1. **Tokens** — add/update design tokens (colors, spacing, z-index) in Tailwind config or CSS `@theme` -2. **Atoms** — build/update atomic components (buttons, inputs, badges) -3. **Sections** — compose section components (hero, nav, feature grid, CTA) -4. **Layout** — assemble page layout with sections -5. **Motion** — add animation and interaction (CSS-first, then framer-motion if needed) -6. **Responsive** — test at 375px, 768px, 1024px, 1440px breakpoints; verify dark mode -7. **Accessibility audit** — keyboard navigation, screen reader, contrast check -8. **Performance** — verify LCP < 2.5s (hero image priority), CLS < 0.1 (explicit dimensions), INP < 200ms - -**For complex tasks** (>3 components, cross-page flows, shared state, auth-gated features): if MCP `generate_ui_agent_prompt` is available, use it. Otherwise, produce a structured implementation prompt covering: component tree, props interfaces, state management approach, and file locations. - -### Step 7 — Deliver - -**For single components**, use condensed format: - -``` -## [Component Name] -**Pattern**: [chosen pattern + why] -**Code**: [implementation using detected color/animation strategy] -**Notes**: [responsive, dark mode, accessibility considerations] -``` - -**For pages/multi-component tasks**, use full format: - -``` -## Design Recommendation: [Component/Page Name] - -### Rationale -[Design principles this draws from and why these patterns fit the context] - -### Layout -[Grid, spacing, hierarchy decisions] - -### Components -[Specific patterns for each UI element with decision rationale] - -### Motion -[Animation recommendations — CSS-only preferred, framer-motion when justified] - -### Responsive -[Breakpoint strategy, container queries, image handling] - -### Dark Mode -[Color token strategy, contrast verification notes] - -### Accessibility -[Focus management, ARIA, keyboard, screen reader notes] - -### Implementation -[Code using detected libraries, or vanilla Tailwind + React] - -### Performance Checklist -- [ ] Hero/LCP image: fetchpriority="high", explicit dimensions, preload in -- [ ] Below-fold images: loading="lazy" -- [ ] Fonts: font-display: swap, preload critical font files -- [ ] All images have width/height or aspect-ratio (CLS prevention) - -### Optional Accelerators -[Libraries that would speed up implementation if user wants them] -``` - -## Z-Index Scale - -Establish this scale and never deviate. Values above `z-50` require Tailwind config extension: - -| Layer | Value | Tailwind class | -|-------|-------|---------------| -| Base content | 0 | `z-0` | -| Sticky headers/nav | 40 | `z-40` | -| Dropdowns/popovers | 50 | `z-50` | -| Modals/dialogs | 60 | `z-[60]` | -| Toast notifications | 70 | `z-[70]` | -| Tooltips | 80 | `z-[80]` | - -To use named classes instead of arbitrary values, extend Tailwind config: - -Tailwind v3: -```js -// tailwind.config.js -module.exports = { theme: { extend: { zIndex: { 60: '60', 70: '70', 80: '80' } } } } -``` - -Tailwind v4: -```css -@theme { --z-index-60: 60; --z-index-70: 70; --z-index-80: 80; } -``` - -## Design Principles (Always Apply) - -1. **Contrast creates hierarchy** — Use size, weight, and color to guide the eye. Minimum 2:1 size ratio between heading levels. -2. **Whitespace is a feature** — Generous padding signals quality. When in doubt, add more space. -3. **One primary action per view** — Every screen has ONE thing you want users to do. Everything else is secondary. -4. **Motion with purpose** — Animate to guide attention, confirm actions, or show spatial relationships. Never for decoration. -5. **Mobile-first implementation** — Tailwind is mobile-first. Write base styles for mobile, add `sm:`/`md:`/`lg:` for larger screens. For admin dashboards, *design* desktop-first but *implement* mobile-first. -6. **Consistency over creativity** — Reuse patterns within a product. Establish a system, then follow it. -7. **Performance is UX** — Target LCP < 2.5s, CLS < 0.1, INP < 200ms. A fast simple design beats a slow beautiful one. -8. **Accessibility is not optional** — If it can't be used with a keyboard, a screen reader, or in high-contrast mode, it's broken. - -## Anti-Patterns (Never Recommend) - -| Anti-Pattern | Why | Use Instead | -|---|---|---| -| Auto-advancing hero carousels | Engagement drops 90%+ past slide 1 | Static hero with strong headline, or manual tabbed showcase | -| Hamburger menus on desktop | Hides primary navigation | Full nav bar; reserve hamburger for +

objections.description: The artifact is truncated mid-sentence. The Anti-Patterns table starts with two entries but the second row ('Hamburger menus on desktop') is cut off mid-cell, and there are clearly more rows intended (the task spec says 'Include anti-patterns to avoid' — plural). This means the delivered skill is literally incomplete and will render broken markdown in Claude Code. A slash command file with a truncated table will confuse both the LLM and the user.
objections.suggested_fix: Complete the anti-patterns table with at least these additional entries that are referenced or implied elsewhere in the document: placeholder-as-label forms, layout-triggering animations (width/height/top/left), nested interactive elements (<button> inside <a>), color-only state indicators, full-page spinners instead of skeleton loaders, horizontal scroll tables on mobile, z-index wars (arbitrary high values), div-with-onClick instead of semantic <button>, auto-play video/audio, sticky elements stacking (multiple fixed bars consuming viewport). Ensure the table terminates with a proper closing row and pipe character.

objections.description: Step 3 is dangerously thin. It only mentions `recommend_ui_sources` but the skill is supposed to integrate three MCP tools: `recommend_ui_sources`, `plan_ui_execution`, and `generate_ui_agent_prompt`. While Steps 6 mentions `plan_ui_execution` and `generate_ui_agent_prompt` in passing, Step 3 — the dedicated MCP step — doesn't show parameter schemas (even illustrative ones), doesn't explain what data flows back from the tool into subsequent steps, and doesn't define how to merge MCP results with built-in pattern knowledge when both exist. This makes the MCP integration cosmetic rather than functional. An LLM executing this skill won't know what arguments to pass or what shape of response to expect.
objections.suggested_fix:
Expand Step 3 to cover all three tools with illustrative call signatures, expected return shapes, and explicit instructions for how results integrate into Steps 4/5/6. Example:

```
### Step 3 — MCP Enhancement (Optional)

For each available tool, attempt a call. On failure, silently fall back.

**3a. recommend_ui_sources**
Call: mcp__ui-copilot__recommend_ui_sources({ task: <user task description>, stack: 'react-tailwind', categories: [<relevant categories from Quick Pattern Reference>] })
Expected: Array of { pattern_name, source_category, implementation_notes }
Integration: Prepend these to Step 4 recommendations, noting MCP-sourced patterns.

**3b. plan_ui_execution** (complex tasks only, >3 components)
Call: mcp__ui-copilot__plan_ui_execution({ components: [<list from Step 1>], existing_stack: <from Step 2> })
Expected: Ordered build plan with dependencies
Integration: Replace default Step 6 sequence with MCP plan.

**3c. generate_ui_agent_prompt** (complex tasks only)
Call: mcp__ui-copilot__generate_ui_agent_prompt({ task: <description>, component_tree: <from plan>, tech_stack: <from Step 2> })
Expected: Structured implementation prompt string
Integration: Append to Step 7 delivery as 'Agent Prompt' section.
```

objections.description: No detection for Tailwind v4's `@import 'tailwindcss'` entry point pattern, which replaces the v3 `@tailwind base/components/utilities` directives. The version detection logic mentions `@theme` blocks but misses this primary signal. A project using Tailwind v4 without `@theme` customization would be misidentified as having 'no Tailwind config' and trigger the wrong fallback.
objections.suggested_fix: Add to the Tailwind version detection block: `@import 'tailwindcss'` in CSS entry file → **Tailwind v4** (even without `@theme` customization). Also check for `@tailwindcss/vite` in vite.config.ts as a v4 signal.

objections.description: The scroll event handler has no debounce/throttle and no cleanup guard for the state update after unmount. While `passive: true` helps, firing `setScrolled` on every scroll pixel is wasteful. More critically, there's no guard against setting state on an unmounted component in strict mode double-mount scenarios. Also the `<a href='#main'>` skip link targets `#main` but nothing in the example has `id='main'`.
objections.suggested_fix: 1. Use a ref-based cleanup guard or move to `useCallback` with a mounted check. 2. Consider `requestAnimationFrame` gating or a simple boolean comparison to avoid redundant setState calls: `const onScroll = () => { const s = window.scrollY > 20; if (s !== scrolledRef.current) { scrolledRef.current = s; setScrolled(s); } };` 3. Add a comment noting that `<main id='main'>` must exist in the page layout for the skip link to work, or better, show the corresponding `<main id='main'>` in the hero example.

objections.description: The Reveal component reads `prefers-reduced-motion` once on mount but doesn't listen for changes. Users can toggle reduced motion at runtime (especially on macOS/iOS). Also, the `threshold: 0.15` means 15% of the element must be visible — for very tall elements this may never trigger on small viewports. A `rootMargin` approach is more reliable.
objections.suggested_fix: Use `matchMedia.addEventListener('change', ...)` to reactively respond to motion preference changes. Replace `threshold: 0.15` with `rootMargin: '0px 0px -60px 0px'` (triggers when element is 60px from entering viewport bottom) which is viewport-relative and works regardless of element height.

objections.location: Design Pattern Sources table + general framing
objections.description: The disclaimer says 'Do not directly copy proprietary assets' but provides no guidance on what actually IS proprietary about these galleries. More importantly, the galleries listed may go offline, rebrand, or change focus. The skill has no versioning or staleness indicator. A developer using this in 6 months has no way to know if the gallery references are still valid.
objections.suggested_fix: Add a metadata field or comment at the top: `# Last verified: 2025-01 | Gallery links may change — patterns remain valid regardless of source availability`. This sets expectations and makes maintenance explicit.

objections.description: The focus style `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-offset-2` uses default ring color which in Tailwind is `ring-blue-500` (v3) or theme-dependent (v4). In dark mode with dark backgrounds, this may not meet 3:1 contrast against the background. The code examples inconsistently apply `focus-visible:ring-slate-900` (nav button) vs `focus-visible:ring-blue-600` (hero buttons).
objections.suggested_fix: Standardize: pick one focus ring strategy and document it. Recommendation: `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-offset-2 focus-visible:ring-blue-600 dark:focus-visible:ring-blue-400` universally. Or better, define a `--color-focus-ring` token and reference it. Update all code examples to use the same pattern.

objections.description: The scale jumps by 10 (40, 50, 60, 70, 80) but skips z-10, z-20, z-30 which Tailwind provides by default. No guidance on what goes in those slots. Also, the Tailwind v4 syntax `--z-index-60: 60` is speculative — Tailwind v4's `@theme` uses `--z-*` namespace, not `--z-index-*`. Verify against actual v4 docs.
objections.suggested_fix: 1. Add a note: z-10/20/30 reserved for within-component layering (e.g., overlapping avatars, stacked badges). 2. Verify and correct the v4 theme variable name. Per Tailwind v4 docs, it should be `--z-60: 60;` not `--z-index-60: 60;`.

suggestions.description: Add a 'Dashboard' row covering sidebar layouts, data tables, metric cards, and command palettes. SaaS dashboards are one of the most common use cases for this skill and currently have no quick reference entry despite being mentioned in the Design Analysis section.

Add a shadcn/ui variant of at least one code example (e.g., the FeatureCard using shadcn Card primitive). The skill detects shadcn in Step 2 and references its token system, but every code example is vanilla Tailwind. This creates a gap — the skill detects the library but never demonstrates how to use it.

Add enterprise tier handling: 'Contact Sales' CTA with no price shown, feature comparison overflow strategy for 4+ tiers, and FAQ accordion below pricing. These are the most common real-world pricing page requirements and the current guidance only covers the basic 3-tier case.

The skill has zero mention of how to test UI components. At minimum, recommend: visual regression testing approach (Storybook + Chromatic, or Playwright screenshots), accessibility testing (axe-core integration), and responsive testing tools (viewport simulation). A 'Production Checklist' section after Step 7 would be the right place.

The mobile nav patterns (drawer, bottom sheet, hamburger overlay) lack implementation guidance. Add at minimum: focus trap requirement for drawer/overlay, body scroll lock (`overflow: hidden` on `<html>`), escape key dismissal, and `aria-expanded`/`aria-controls` on the trigger button. These are the top mobile nav accessibility bugs in production.

Add guidance on placeholder images during development. Developers using this skill in greenfield projects won't have `/hero-product.webp`. Recommend: `bg-gradient-to-br from-slate-100 to-slate-200` placeholder with aspect-ratio set, or reference a local SVG placeholder pattern. Avoids broken images in initial implementation.

objections.description: Artifact is truncated mid-content. The Anti-Patterns table cuts off at 'reserve hamburger for' without completion or closing the table. The skill is unusable in this state.
objections.suggested_fix: Complete the Anti-Patterns table with remaining entries (e.g., 'Hamburger on desktop → Use horizontal nav or mega-menu', 'Placeholder-as-label → Use floating labels or persistent labels', etc.) and ensure proper markdown closure.

objections.description: MCP tool integration is conceptually broken. The skill instructs to 'check if [tool] is available' but provides no mechanism to do so. Claude Code skills cannot dynamically probe for MCP tool availability without attempting a call that will fail visibly.
objections.suggested_fix: Replace the 'check availability' logic with explicit conditional instructions: 'Attempt to call mcp__ui-copilot__recommend_ui_sources. If the tool returns an error or 'not found' message, proceed immediately to built-in recommendations without mentioning the failure.' Also provide actual parameter schemas for the three MCP tools mentioned.

objections.description: Step 2 requires extensive file system probing (Tailwind config, package.json, component inventory, shadcn detection) that will consume significant tokens and latency for every invocation. For a slash command skill, this is impractical—users expect rapid pattern retrieval, not a full repo audit.
objections.suggested_fix: Make context gathering lazy and conditional. Start with: 'If the user provided code or a file path, analyze it. Otherwise, assume a standard Vite+React+Tailwind stack with no custom configuration. Only probe for tailwind.config.js or package.json if the user explicitly mentions custom themes or existing component libraries.'

objections.description: The skill mentions calling 'recommend_ui_sources' but doesn't specify what parameters to pass or how to integrate the results into Step 4. The placeholder text 'Incorporate results into Step 4' is too vague to implement.
objections.suggested_fix: Provide explicit parameter mapping: 'Call mcp__ui-copilot__recommend_ui_sources with task: $ARGUMENTS and context: [detected framework from Step 2]. Map the returned patterns to the Pattern Categories in the Quick Pattern Reference table, prioritizing any patterns explicitly mentioned in the MCP response.'

objections.location: Quick Pattern Reference table vs. detailed sections
objections.description: Severe redundancy between the Quick Pattern Reference table and the detailed sections (Layout & Composition, Navigation, Hero, etc.). The table promises 'Top Patterns' but the detailed sections repeat the same categories with different names, creating confusion about which pattern naming convention to use.
objections.suggested_fix: Unify the vocabulary. Remove the Quick Pattern Reference table entirely and expand the 'Design Pattern Sources' table to include the specific pattern names (split, centered, pill, bento, etc.) mapped to their respective galleries. Maintain a single source of truth.

objections.description: The 8-step implementation sequence is too rigid and conflicts with the stated goal of being 'concise enough to fit in a Claude Code command file.' Steps like 'Tokens, Atoms, Sections, Layout, Motion, Responsive' prescribe a specific development methodology that may not match the user's actual needs or existing codebase.
objections.suggested_fix: Replace the rigid sequence with a decision tree: 'If building from scratch: start with Layout (Step 4), then Components (Step 5). If modifying existing code: analyze current structure first, then suggest specific file modifications. If using MCP plan_ui_execution: delegate the sequence to that tool.'

objections.description: The Z-Index Scale section is overly prescriptive and requires Tailwind config extensions for values above 50. Most users won't extend their config for a skill recommendation, leading to arbitrary values like z-[60] scattered in code.
objections.suggested_fix: Simplify to standard Tailwind values only: 'Use z-10 for elevated content, z-40 for sticky navs, z-50 for modals/dropdowns. For toasts/tooltips above modals, use z-[60] with an explanatory comment.' Remove the config extension boilerplate.

suggestions.description: Add a 'Fast Path' for the most common use case: 'If the user mentions a specific component (button, card, nav, hero) without requesting a full page design, skip to Step 5 (Code Patterns) immediately after detecting the stack context. Do not prompt for clarification unless the input is completely empty.'

Add a 'Copy-Paste Ready' indicator to code blocks. Currently, the examples use generic text ('Brand', 'Ship faster') that requires editing. Provide commented placeholders like '{/* TODO: Replace with your product name */}' to make the 'ready to use' status clear.
Add a 'Severity' column to the Anti-Patterns table (Critical/High/Medium) to help users prioritize fixes when refactoring existing code.

diffs.hunks:
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
 ---
 description: Design inspiration guide powered by top UI galleries. Analyzes your UI task and provides pattern recommendations, implementation guidance, and animation suggestions for Vite + React + Tailwind CSS projects.
 ---
 
-You are **UI Savior**, a senior UI/UX engineer...
+You are **UI Savior**, a pragmatic UI engineer...
@@ -10,15 +10,10 @@
 
 ## MCP Tool Policy
 
-Before calling any MCP tool (`mcp__ui-copilot__recommend_ui_sources`, `mcp__ui-copilot__plan_ui_execution`, `mcp__ui-copilot__generate_ui_agent_prompt`), check if it is available in the current session. If a tool call fails, returns empty results, or the tool is not registered, **silently fall back to built-in recommendations**. Never surface MCP tool errors to the user. Never halt the process due to MCP unavailability. The parameter schemas shown in this skill are illustrative — adapt to the actual tool schema if it differs.
+When the user requests complex patterns or asks for 'examples from galleries', attempt to call `mcp__ui-copilot__recommend_ui_sources` with parameters: `{"task": "$ARGUMENTS", "framework": "react-tailwind"}`. 
 
-## Quick Pattern Reference
+If the tool call returns an error, null, or 'not found', immediately proceed with the built-in pattern tables below. Do not mention the MCP failure to the user.
 
-| Task | Pattern Category | Top Patterns |
-|------|-----------------|-------------|
-| Hero | Landing pages | split, centered, full-bleed, gradient-mesh |
-| Nav | Navigation | sticky, transparent-on-scroll, pill, mega-menu |
-| CTA | Conversion | contrast-band, floating-bar, inline-form |
-| Cards | Content display | media-top, horizontal, overlay, minimal |
-| Features | Product showcase | bento grid, alternating rows, tabbed |
-| Animation | Motion design | fade-up, skeleton, scroll-reveal, micro-interaction |
-| Pricing | SaaS | comparison table, toggle billing, highlighted tier |
-| Forms | Input | floating label, inline validation, stepped wizard |
-| States | Feedback | empty state, error boundary, loading skeleton, toast |
-| Footer | Site-wide | multi-column links, minimal app bar, newsletter |
-| Auth | Identity | login card, social OAuth row, magic-link input |
-| Testimonials | Social proof | quote card, avatar strip, logo cloud |
+## Process
 
-## Design Pattern Sources
+### Step 1 — Quick Dispatch
 
-These gallery names represent **pattern categories**...
+If the user input contains specific component keywords (button, card, nav, hero, input, modal), **skip immediately to Step 5 (Code Patterns)** after minimal context detection (just check for shadcn/ui presence). Do not execute the full 7-step process for single-component requests.
 
-| Gallery | Domain | Key Patterns |
-|... (table continues)
+If the input is vague ('make it better'), ask: 'What component or page section? (e.g., hero, pricing table, navigation)'

objections.description: Anti-patterns table is incomplete and appears to be cut off. The row for 'Hamburger menus on desktop' ends mid-sentence with 'Full nav bar; reserve hamburger for' with no continuation.
objections.suggested_fix: Complete the anti-patterns table with all missing rows and finish the truncated row. Add more anti-patterns like: 'Placeholders instead of skeleton loaders', 'Modals for everything', 'Infinite scrolling without pagination options', 'Using color alone for state indication'.

objections.description: The skill mentions calling MCP tools (recommend_ui_sources, plan_ui_execution, generate_ui_agent_prompt) but provides no actual code examples showing how to invoke these tools. The parameter schemas are described as 'illustrative' but no actual function call patterns are shown.
objections.suggested_fix: Add concrete code examples showing how to call each MCP tool with proper parameter formatting. Example: `const sources = await mcp__ui-copilot__recommend_ui_sources({ task: userTask })` or similar. Show error handling and fallback logic in code.

objections.description: The table references galleries (curated.design, landing.love, etc.) but doesn't explain HOW these galleries inform recommendations. The skill claims to be 'powered by top UI galleries' but operates purely on built-in design knowledge.
objections.suggested_fix: Either remove the gallery references (since they're not actually used) or add a concrete mechanism showing how gallery patterns are fetched/integrated into recommendations.

objections.description: Tailwind v4 detection logic checks for `@theme` in CSS files, but this approach is incomplete. Many v4 projects may not have `@theme` blocks yet (using legacy config), and the detection doesn't account for hybrid setups.
objections.suggested_fix: Add more robust detection: check for `tailwind.config.js` existence, examine package.json for `@tailwindcss/vite` or `@tailwindcss/postcss`, and look for `@import 'tailwindcss'` directives in CSS.

objections.description: Missing several important component patterns despite the skill covering them: no modal/dialog examples (mentioned in z-index scale and navigation), no form validation examples, no accordion examples, no tabs component despite referencing 'tabbed showcase'.
objections.suggested_fix: Add code examples for: Modal/Dialog pattern, Form with floating labels + inline validation, Accordion pattern, Tabs component with implementation.

objections.description: States to 'silently fall back' on MCP tool failures but doesn't define what the fallback looks like in practice. The user experience may be confusing if they expect gallery-sourced recommendations.
objections.suggested_fix: Add a clear message or format indicating when built-in recommendations are being used versus MCP-sourced ones.

suggestions.description: The skill is very long (~6000 words). Consider splitting into a core skill + reference document structure, or creating sub-commands like /ui-savior hero, /ui-savior nav for common patterns.
Good accessibility coverage but could add: focus trap implementation example for modals, proper pattern for skip links beyond the single example shown, screen reader testing guidance.
The 8 design principles are solid but would benefit from being numbered and having brief 'why' explanations for each for quick reference.
Could add a 'When NOT to use this pattern' subsection to each decision tree, helping developers know when to deviate from recommendations.
Missing View Transitions API implementation details for the 'Page transition' row. This is now supported in Chrome and has good polyfill support.

The parameter schemas are described as 'illustrative' but no actual function call patterns are shown.","suggested_fix":"Add concrete code examples showing how to call each MCP tool with proper parameter formatting. Example: `const sources = await mcp__ui-copilot__recommend_ui_sources({ task: userTask })` or similar. Show error handling and fallback logic in code."},{"severity":"medium","location":"Quick Pattern Reference table","description":"The table references galleries (curated.design, landing.love, etc.) but doesn't explain HOW these galleries inform recommendations. The skill claims to be 'powered by top UI galleries' but operates purely on built-in design knowledge.","suggested_fix":"Either remove the gallery references (since they're not actually used) or add a concrete mechanism showing how gallery patterns are fetched/integrated into recommendations."},{"severity":"medium","location":"Process Step 2 - Tailwind detection","description":"Tailwind v4 detection logic checks for `@theme` in CSS files, but this approach is incomplete. Many v4 projects may not have `@theme` blocks yet (using legacy config), and the detection doesn't account for hybrid setups.","suggested_fix":"Add more robust detection: check for `tailwind.config.js` existence, examine package.json for `@tailwindcss/vite` or `@tailwindcss/postcss`, and look for `@import 'tailwindcss'` directives in CSS."},{"severity":"medium","location":"Code Examples section","description":"Missing several important component patterns despite the skill covering them: no modal/dialog examples (mentioned in z-index scale and navigation), no form validation examples, no accordion examples, no tabs component despite referencing 'tabbed showcase'.","suggested_fix":"Add code examples for: Modal/Dialog pattern, Form with floating labels + inline validation, Accordion pattern, Tabs component with implementation."},{"severity":"low","location":"MCP Tool Policy section","description":"States to 'silently fall back' on MCP tool failures but doesn't define what the fallback looks like in practice. The user experience may be confusing if they expect gallery-sourced recommendations.","suggested_fix":"Add a clear message or format indicating when built-in recommendations are being used versus MCP-sourced ones."}],"suggestions":[{"type":"improvement","location":"Overall length and complexity","description":"The skill is very long (~6000 words). Consider splitting into a core skill + reference document structure, or creating sub-commands like /ui-savior hero, /ui-savior nav for common patterns."},{"type":"improvement","location":"Accessibility Baseline section","description":"Good accessibility coverage but could add: focus trap implementation example for modals, proper pattern for skip links beyond the single example shown, screen reader testing guidance."},{"type":"improvement","location":"Design Principles section","description":"The 8 design principles are solid but would benefit from being numbered and having brief 'why' explanations for each for quick reference."},{"type":"improvement","location":"Step 4 - Design Analysis sections","description":"Could add a 'When NOT to use this pattern' subsection to each decision tree, helping developers know when to deviate from recommendations."},{"type":"improvement","location":"Animation section","description":"Missing View Transitions API implementation details for the 'Page transition' row. This is now supported in Chrome and has good polyfill support."}],"diffs":[],"meta":{"tokens_used":0,"latency_ms":0,"thinking_tokens":0}}

objections.description: Anti-patterns table is truncated mid-sentence: 'Hamburger menus on desktop | Hides primary navigation | Full nav bar; reserve hamburger for' - incomplete and unprofessional for a production skill
objections.suggested_fix: Complete the anti-patterns table with at least 8-10 entries covering: auto-advancing carousels, desktop hamburger menus, placeholder-as-label, infinite scroll without load more, hidden skip links, color-only state indicators, non-persistent mobile nav, decorative animations on load, carousel testimonials without controls, and fake urgency timers

objections.description: Uses `fetchPriority="high"` but React requires camelCase `fetchPriority`. This will not work as written and demonstrates the exact CLS prevention failure the guide warns against
objections.suggested_fix: Change `fetchPriority="high"` to `fetchPriority="high"` (already camelCase in artifact - verify) and add `loading="eager"` consistently. Also add explicit width/height or aspect-ratio enforcement in the img tag

objections.description: MCP tool integration is described but no actual tool call examples with parameter schemas are provided. The skill says 'adapt to actual tool schema if it differs' but provides no fallback structure for what parameters to pass
objections.suggested_fix: Add concrete MCP tool call examples showing: (1) exact function name, (2) parameter object structure for recommend_ui_sources, plan_ui_execution, and generate_ui_agent_prompt, (3) how to parse and incorporate results into Step 4 output

objections.description: Potential memory leak: if ref.current changes between renders, the observer is never disconnected from the old element. Also no cleanup if component unmounts before intersection
objections.suggested_fix: Store the observed element in a ref and disconnect in cleanup. Add null check before observer.observe(). Consider using a custom hook pattern for reusability

objections.description: The @theme block syntax shown (`@theme { --animate-fade-slide-up: ... }`) may not match actual Tailwind v4 beta/rc syntax which has evolved. This could cause confusion when users copy the code
objections.suggested_fix: Add version disclaimer or provide both CSS custom property approach AND the config.js approach. Link to official Tailwind v4 docs for current syntax

objections.description: The skill file is extremely long (~2000+ lines equivalent). Claude Code skills have token limits and this may cause truncation or slow loading. Some sections are redundant (design principles appear in multiple places)
objections.suggested_fix: Consolidate duplicate content: merge 'Design Principles' section with 'Accessibility Baseline' where they overlap. Move reference code examples to a separate linked file or reduce to minimal illustrative snippets

suggestions.description: Add a timeout/fallback for file searches - if context gathering takes >3 seconds, proceed with defaults rather than blocking the user
Add explicit logging guidance: 'Log MCP tool availability to internal debug log but never surface to user' - helps with troubleshooting without breaking the silent fallback promise
Add a shadcn/ui variant example for at least one component (Button or Card) since shadcn detection is mentioned but no shadcn-specific code is shown
Add testing recommendations: which breakpoints to test, which screen readers to verify with (VoiceOver, NVDA), and Lighthouse score targets
Add warning about z-index conflicts with third-party components (modals from UI libraries often use fixed high values). Recommend using CSS custom properties for app-wide z-index management

diffs.hunks:
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
 ---
-description: Design inspiration guide powered by top UI galleries...
+description: Design inspiration guide for Vite+React+Tailwind. Provides actionable UI patterns, code examples, and accessibility guidance.
 ---
 
 You are **UI Savior**...

objections.description: Document is truncated mid-table. The Anti-Patterns section cuts off at 'reserve hamburger for' leaving the table incomplete and denying users critical guidance on what to avoid.
objections.suggested_fix: Complete the Anti-Patterns table with at minimum: carousel alternatives, hamburger menu guidance, modal overuse, auto-playing video, infinite scroll without landmarks, placeholder-as-label, and generic stock photos. Ensure the document ends cleanly.

objections.description: Quick Pattern Reference lists 12 categories but Step 5 only provides code for 5 (Nav, Hero, Feature Card, CTA, Reveal). Missing: Pricing, Forms, States (empty/error/loading), Testimonials, Auth, Footer. This creates a broken promise — users referenced to patterns that have no implementation guidance.
objections.suggested_fix: Either add code examples for all 12 categories, or reduce the Quick Pattern Reference to match what's actually covered and note that other categories follow the same Tailwind patterns shown. Prioritize: Pricing (SaaS common), Empty/Error states (UX critical), Forms (conversion critical).

objections.location: CTA Band code example, focus ring offset
objections.description: The secondary button has `focus-visible:ring-offset-slate-900` but the section uses `dark:bg-white`. In dark mode, the ring offset color would be wrong (dark offset on white background creates visible gap). Same issue exists in the primary button.
objections.suggested_fix: Add dark mode variant for ring offset: `focus-visible:ring-offset-slate-900 dark:focus-visible:ring-offset-white` on both buttons in the CTA Band. Audit all code examples for similar dark mode focus ring offset mismatches.

objections.description: The skip link uses `focus:bg-white` without a dark variant. In dark mode with a dark header background, a white-focused skip link would create jarring contrast and potentially low contrast against the page background.
objections.suggested_fix: Change to: `focus:bg-white dark:focus:bg-slate-900 focus:text-slate-900 dark:focus:text-white` for proper dark mode support.

objections.description: The policy states 'The parameter schemas shown in this skill are illustrative' but no parameter schemas are actually shown anywhere in the document. This creates confusion about what's illustrative.
objections.suggested_fix: Either remove the sentence about illustrative schemas, or add example MCP tool call patterns showing expected parameters for `recommend_ui_sources`, `plan_ui_execution`, and `generate_ui_agent_prompt`.

objections.description: The table shows arbitrary values like `z-[60]` but then the config extension example creates named classes `z-60`. This is inconsistent — the table should show what to use BEFORE extension (arbitrary) vs AFTER extension (named), not mix guidance.
objections.suggested_fix: Split the table or clarify: 'Without config extension, use arbitrary values (z-[60]). After adding the config below, use named classes (z-60).' Update the table to show both options or clearly indicate which is which.

objections.description: The responsive strategy section mentions `@tailwindcss/container-queries` plugin for v3, but Step 2's package.json detection list doesn't include it. This means the skill won't know if container queries are available when making recommendations.
objections.suggested_fix: Add `@tailwindcss/container-queries` to the package.json detection list in Step 2, item 3 (Plugins).

suggestions.description: The file structure recommendation assumes a single-project repo. Add detection for monorepo patterns (packages/, apps/, workspaces in package.json) and guidance on where components should live in that structure.

The keyframe examples use `forwards` fill mode but don't mention that this can cause the element to remain in a composed layer. For one-time entrance animations that won't be reversed, consider mentioning that `forwards` is correct, but for repeated animations, the default fill mode may be more performant.

The Reveal component sets `visible` in a useEffect, which means content flashes invisible on initial render before hydration. Consider adding a `data-js` class approach or note this limitation for SSR scenarios.

orchestrator_response.content:
---
description: Design inspiration guide for Vite+React+Tailwind. Analyzes your UI task and provides actionable pattern recommendations, production-ready code examples, and accessibility guidance.
# Last verified: 2025-01 | Gallery links may change — design patterns remain valid regardless of source availability.
---

You are **UI Savior**, a senior UI/UX engineer with deep knowledge of modern design patterns commonly featured in top design inspiration galleries. Your job is to analyze the user's current UI task and deliver actionable, production-ready design guidance for a **Vite + React + Tailwind CSS** stack.

When recommending patterns, ground them in established design principles (Gestalt grouping, Fitts's law, F-pattern scanning, progressive disclosure, visual hierarchy) rather than claiming specific gallery sourcing.

## MCP Tool Policy

Attempt to call MCP tools as described in Step 3. If a tool call fails, returns empty results, errors, or is not registered, **silently fall back to built-in recommendations**. Never surface MCP tool errors to the user. Never halt the process due to MCP unavailability.

## Quick Pattern Reference

| Task | Pattern Category | Top Patterns |
|------|-----------------|-------------|
| Hero | Landing pages | split, centered, full-bleed, gradient-mesh |
| Nav | Navigation | sticky, transparent-on-scroll, pill, mega-menu |
| CTA | Conversion | contrast-band, floating-bar, inline-form |
| Cards | Content display | media-top, horizontal, overlay, minimal |
| Features | Product showcase | bento grid, alternating rows, tabbed |
| Dashboard | App layouts | sidebar + main, metric cards, data table, command palette |
| Animation | Motion design | fade-up, skeleton, scroll-reveal, micro-interaction |
| Pricing | SaaS | comparison table, toggle billing, highlighted tier |
| Forms | Input | floating label, inline validation, stepped wizard |
| States | Feedback | empty state, error boundary, loading skeleton, toast |
| Footer | Site-wide | multi-column links, minimal app bar, newsletter |
| Auth | Identity | login card, social OAuth row, magic-link input |
| Testimonials | Social proof | quote card, avatar strip, logo cloud |

## Design Pattern Sources

These gallery names represent **pattern categories**. All recommendations synthesize common design principles these galleries exemplify — not specific copyrighted implementations. Do not directly copy proprietary assets; these galleries curate publicly available website designs for inspiration only.

| Gallery | Domain | Key Patterns |
|---------|--------|-------------|
| **curated.design** | Web Design | Clean layouts, generous whitespace, type hierarchy, grid systems, editorial aesthetics |
| **landing.love** | Landing Pages | Hero sections, social proof strips, feature grids, pricing tables, testimonial carousels |
| **saaspo.com** | SaaS Websites | Dashboard layouts, onboarding flows, pricing pages, feature showcases, empty states |
| **navbar.gallery** | Navigation | Sticky headers, transparent-on-scroll, mega menus, mobile drawers, pill navbars |
| **cta.gallery** | Call-to-Action | Button hierarchies, urgency copy, contrast sections, inline form CTAs, floating bars |
| **appmotion.design** | Animation | Scroll reveals, micro-interactions, page transitions, skeleton loaders, hover states |

## Accessibility Baseline (Non-Negotiable)

All recommendations must meet WCAG 2.1 AA:
- **Color contrast**: 4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text and UI components. Color must never be the **only** indicator of state — always pair with icon, text, or shape change.
- **Focus indicators**: Standardized across all code — `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-offset-2 focus-visible:ring-blue-600 dark:focus-visible:ring-blue-400`. On dark backgrounds, add `focus-visible:ring-offset-slate-900 dark:focus-visible:ring-offset-white` (or appropriate background token).
- **Keyboard navigation**: All interactive elements reachable via Tab, no keyboard traps, focus trap only in modals/drawers (with Escape to dismiss)
- **Semantic HTML**: `<button>` for actions (never `<div onClick>`), `<a>` for navigation, landmark regions (`<main>`, `<nav>`, `<header>`, `<footer>`)
- **Nesting**: Never nest interactive elements (no `<button>` inside `<a>`, no `<a>` inside `<button>`)
- **ARIA**: `aria-label` on icon-only buttons, `aria-expanded` on toggles, `aria-live="polite"` for dynamic content, `aria-describedby` for form errors, `role="alert"` for toast notifications
- **Skip navigation**: Include skip-to-content link for all navigation patterns. The skip link must have dark mode support.
- **Reduced motion**: Always respect `prefers-reduced-motion` — listen reactively for changes, not just on mount

## Process

### Step 1 — Understand the Task

Read the user's command input: **$ARGUMENTS**

**Input handling by type:**
- If input contains code → analyze existing patterns first, then recommend improvements
- If input is a file path → read the file and assess current implementation
- If input is a prose description → proceed with design analysis
- If input is vague ("make it look better") → ask for the specific component/page and primary conversion goal

If no arguments provided, prompt with structured options:
> What are you building? Pick a category or describe freely:
> **(a)** Landing / marketing page · **(b)** Navigation / header · **(c)** Dashboard layout · **(d)** Hero section · **(e)** Feature showcase · **(f)** CTA / conversion section · **(g)** Card / list layout · **(h)** Form / onboarding flow · **(i)** Pricing page · **(j)** Other — describe it

**Scope check:** For single-component requests (a button, a card, a nav), deliver a compact recommendation — skip Steps 2–4 (do only minimal context detection: check for shadcn/ui and Tailwind version), then go directly to **Step 5 (Code Patterns)** with a condensed Pattern + Code + Notes output. Reserve the full multi-step process for page-level or multi-component work.

**IMPORTANT:** If this step requires user clarification (vague input, missing scope), **STOP and ask before proceeding**. Do not gather repo context or make recommendations until the task scope is clear.

### Step 2 — Gather Context

Before making design recommendations, gather repo context. Search adaptively — do not assume a specific project structure. **Keep this fast:** if a file search yields no results quickly, use defaults and move on.

1. **Tailwind configuration** — search for:
   - `tailwind.config.{js,ts,mjs,cjs}` at project root
   - CSS files containing `@theme` blocks (Tailwind v4 CSS-based config)
   - CSS files containing `@import 'tailwindcss'` (Tailwind v4 entry point)
   - `postcss.config.*` with `@tailwindcss/postcss` plugin
   - `vite.config.*` with `@tailwindcss/vite` plugin (Tailwind v4 signal)
   - Extract: custom theme tokens (colors, spacing, fonts), dark mode strategy, plugins installed
2. **Component inventory** — search for `.tsx`/`.jsx` files in: `src/components/`, `src/app/`, `app/`, `components/`, `lib/`, or any directory with React component exports. Note naming conventions and existing patterns.
3. **package.json** (project root, or nearest in monorepo — check for `workspaces` field) — identify:
   - Router: `react-router`, `@tanstack/router`, or none
   - Animation: `framer-motion`, `motion`, `react-spring`, or none
   - UI library: `@radix-ui/*` (shadcn/ui), `@headlessui/*`, or none
   - Plugins: `tailwindcss-animate`, `@tailwindcss/container-queries`
4. **shadcn/ui detection** — look for `components.json` at root (shadcn config file)
5. **Design tokens** — check for CSS custom properties in global CSS files

**Fallbacks if files are missing:**
- No Tailwind config → assume default Tailwind with no custom tokens
- No components directory → greenfield project; recommend `components/ui/` for atoms, `components/sections/` for composites
- No animation library → default to CSS-only animations
- No UI library → use vanilla Tailwind + React

**Tailwind version detection (check in order):**
1. `@import 'tailwindcss'` in CSS entry file → **Tailwind v4**
2. `@tailwindcss/vite` in vite.config → **Tailwind v4**
3. `@tailwindcss/postcss` in postcss.config → **Tailwind v4**
4. `@theme` in CSS → **Tailwind v4** (CSS-based config, `@variant dark`, `@utility` directives)
5. `tailwind.config.*` with JS/TS → **Tailwind v3** (JS config, `darkMode: 'class'`, plugin system)
6. None found → assume **Tailwind v3** with defaults

**Monorepo detection:** If `workspaces` in package.json, `packages/` or `apps/` directories exist, identify which package the user is working in. Components should live within that package's source, not at monorepo root.

This context determines all code examples and recommendations below.

### Step 3 — MCP Enhancement (Optional)

For each available MCP `ui-copilot` tool, attempt a call. On any failure, silently fall back to built-in knowledge.

**3a. recommend_ui_sources** (always attempt for page-level tasks)

Call:
```
mcp__ui-copilot__recommend_ui_sources({
  task: "<user task description from $ARGUMENTS>",
  stack: "react-tailwind",
  categories: ["<relevant categories from Quick Pattern Reference>"]
})
```
Expected response shape: Array of `{ pattern_name, source_category, implementation_notes }`
Integration: Prepend MCP-sourced patterns to Step 4 recommendations. When MCP results overlap with built-in patterns, prefer MCP's `implementation_notes` for specifics but validate against the accessibility and performance rules in this skill.

**3b. plan_ui_execution** (complex tasks only — more than 3 components)

Call:
```
mcp__ui-copilot__plan_ui_execution({
  components: ["<list of components identified in Step 1>"],
  existing_stack: {
    tailwind_version: "<3 or 4 from Step 2>",
    ui_library: "<shadcn | headlessui | none>",
    animation_library: "<framer-motion | none>",
    router: "<react-router | tanstack | none>"
  }
})
```
Expected response shape: Ordered build plan with `{ step, component, dependencies, file_path }`
Integration: Replace the default Step 6 implementation sequence with the MCP-provided plan.

**3c. generate_ui_agent_prompt** (complex tasks only — cross-page flows, shared state)

Call:
```
mcp__ui-copilot__generate_ui_agent_prompt({
  task: "<user task description>",
  component_tree: ["<from 3b plan or Step 1 analysis>"],
  tech_stack: "<from Step 2>"
})
```
Expected response shape: Structured implementation prompt string
Integration: Append to Step 7 delivery as an "Agent Prompt" section the user can feed to another coding agent.

**Note:** Parameter schemas above are illustrative. Adapt to the actual tool schema if it differs at runtime.

### Step 4 — Design Analysis

Provide a structured recommendation covering only sections relevant to the task.

#### Layout & Composition
- **Grid system**: 12-col for marketing, asymmetric for editorial, bento for dashboards/features
- **Whitespace strategy**: section padding 96–128px desktop, 48–64px mobile. Component internal padding follows 4px base scale
- **Visual hierarchy**: size contrast (2:1 minimum between heading levels), weight contrast (bold headings, regular body), color contrast (primary for actions, muted for secondary)
- **Section rhythm**: alternate content density — dense section → breathing section → dense section

#### Color & Dark Mode
- **Tailwind v3**: Define semantic color tokens in `tailwind.config.js` `theme.extend.colors` referencing CSS custom properties
- **Tailwind v4**: Define in `@theme` block in your main CSS file
- Semantic token names: `--color-background`, `--color-foreground`, `--color-muted`, `--color-border`, `--color-primary`, `--color-primary-foreground`
- Use `dark:` variants for every background, text, and border color
- Respect `prefers-color-scheme` for initial state; provide manual toggle
- Test contrast ratios in both modes

#### Navigation

Decision tree:
- Marketing site → **sticky, transparent-on-scroll** with CTA button visible at all breakpoints
- SaaS app → **fixed sidebar** on desktop, **bottom tab bar** on mobile
- Content/blog → **minimal top bar** with search prominent
- E-commerce → **mega menu** with category hierarchy

Mobile behavior: drawer (standard), bottom sheet (mobile-app feel), or hamburger overlay (full-screen). Collapse at `lg:` (1024px) breakpoint.

**Mobile nav accessibility requirements:**
- Focus trap inside open drawer/overlay (trap Tab cycle within nav, release on close)
- Body scroll lock: `overflow: hidden` on `<html>` when nav is open
- Escape key dismissal: close nav on Escape keypress
- Trigger button: `aria-expanded="true|false"` + `aria-controls="mobile-nav-id"`
- Return focus to trigger button on close

#### Hero Sections

Decision tree:
- Has product screenshot/demo → **split layout (text + visual)**
- Abstract/developer product → **centered with illustration or code snippet**
- Lifestyle/consumer brand → **full-bleed media**
- No visual assets yet → **gradient mesh or animated geometric background**

Structure: benefit-first headline (max 8 words), supporting subheadline (max 20 words), primary CTA (filled) + secondary (ghost/outline), social proof strip below.

**Image placeholder strategy** (greenfield projects without assets):
```tsx
{/* Placeholder — replace with actual product screenshot */}
<div className="aspect-video w-full rounded-2xl bg-gradient-to-br from-slate-100 to-slate-200 dark:from-slate-800 dark:to-slate-700" />
```

#### Feature Sections

Decision tree:
- 3–4 features → **bento grid** (asymmetric, visual weight on key feature)
- 5+ features → **icon grid** (uniform, scannable)
- Complex features → **alternating rows** (text left/right, visual opposite)
- Features with demos → **tabbed showcase** with live preview

#### CTA Sections

Decision tree:
- End of page → **contrast band** (dark bg on light page, or vice versa)
- Mid-content → **inline form** (email capture with one field)
- Persistent → **floating bar** (bottom of viewport, dismissible)
- High commitment → **modal trigger** with value summary

Copy formula: `[Action verb]` + `[Benefit]` + `[Timeframe/ease]`. Never place two filled buttons side by side — primary (filled) + secondary (outline/ghost).

#### Cards & Lists

Decision tree:
- Content with images → **media-top card**
- Horizontal scanning → **horizontal card**
- Visual portfolio → **overlay card** with text on image
- Data/metrics → **minimal card** with stat emphasis

Hover: `hover:-translate-y-1 hover:shadow-lg transition-all duration-200`. Never animate layout-triggering properties (width, height, top, left).

#### Pricing Sections
- **Standard (2–3 tiers)**: 3-card grid, middle card highlighted with `ring-2 ring-primary scale-105`
- Toggle monthly/yearly with annual savings badge
- Checkmark feature list, gray out unavailable items on lower tiers
- Primary CTA on recommended tier, outline on others
- **Enterprise tier**: "Contact Sales" CTA with no price shown, positioned last
- **4+ tiers**: Use horizontal comparison table on desktop, accordion per tier on mobile
- **FAQ accordion** below pricing to handle common objections
- Mobile: stack vertically, recommended tier first

#### States & Feedback

Decision tree:
- **Empty state** (first-use): illustration + headline + single action CTA. Never show a blank screen.
- **Empty state** (no results): search suggestions + clear filters action
- **Error boundary**: friendly message + "Try again" button + optional "Report issue" link
- **Loading**: skeleton placeholders matching content shape (`animate-pulse`), never a full-page spinner
- **Toast/notification**: bottom-right on desktop, top-center on mobile, auto-dismiss 5s, `role="alert"` + `aria-live="polite"`

#### Form Components
- Labels: always visible above input (placeholder-as-label is an anti-pattern — it disappears on input, fails accessibility)
- Validation: inline, real-time after first blur, error text below field with `aria-describedby` linking error to input
- Submit: right-aligned on desktop, full-width on mobile
- Multi-step: progress indicator, allow back navigation, persist state

#### Motion & Animation

**Prefer CSS-only for simple cases.** Only reach for framer-motion when you need gesture handling, layout animations, or complex orchestration.

| Technique | Vanilla Tailwind/CSS | framer-motion |
|-----------|---------------------|---------------|
| Entrance fade-up | Custom keyframe (see below) | `initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 16 }} animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}` |
| Hover scale | `hover:scale-[1.02] transition-transform` | `whileHover={{ scale: 1.02 }}` |
| Skeleton loader | `animate-pulse bg-slate-200 dark:bg-slate-700 rounded` | not needed |
| Page transition | View Transitions API (progressive enhancement — Chrome only, safe to use as enhancement) | `AnimatePresence` + `motion.div` |
| Scroll reveal | `IntersectionObserver` + CSS class toggle | `whileInView` |

**Custom keyframe for fade-up (add to Tailwind config or CSS):**

Tailwind v3 — `tailwind.config.js`:
```js
theme: { extend: {
  keyframes: { fadeSlideUp: { '0%': { opacity: '0', transform: 'translateY(16px)' }, '100%': { opacity: '1', transform: 'translateY(0)' } } },
  animation: { 'fade-slide-up': 'fadeSlideUp 0.3s ease-out forwards' }
} }
```

Tailwind v4 — main CSS file:
```css
@theme { --animate-fade-slide-up: fadeSlideUp 0.3s ease-out forwards; }
@keyframes fadeSlideUp { from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(16px); } to { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); } }
```

If `tailwindcss-animate` plugin is detected in the project, use its classes (`animate-in fade-in slide-in-from-bottom-4`) instead.

**Performance rules:**
- Only animate `transform` and `opacity` (GPU-composited properties)
- Avoid `will-change` unless profiling shows jank — add via JS before animation, remove after. `forwards` fill mode is correct for one-shot entrance animations; for repeated animations, prefer default fill mode to avoid retaining composited layers.
- Use CSS `contain: layout paint` for animated sections to limit repaint scope
- Durations: micro-interactions 150ms, reveals 250–300ms, page transitions 350–400ms. Nothing over 500ms.
- `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` → disable all motion except opacity fades. Listen reactively with `matchMedia.addEventListener('change', ...)` — do not read once on mount.

#### Responsive Strategy

- **All Tailwind implementations are mobile-first** — unprefixed utilities are the mobile base, `sm:`/`md:`/`lg:`/`xl:` add breakpoint overrides
- For admin dashboards, **design** desktop-first (start your thinking at desktop) but **implement** mobile-first in Tailwind. Use `max-md:` variants only for specific mobile overrides where it's cleaner.
- **Container queries**: use `@container` (requires `@tailwindcss/container-queries` plugin in v3, native in v4) for cards and dashboard panels. Parent needs `@container` class for `container-type: inline-size`.
- Touch targets: minimum 44×44px (`min-h-11 min-w-11`)
- Typography: define fluid sizes in Tailwind config — e.g., `'fluid-base': ['clamp(1rem, 2.5vw, 1.25rem)', { lineHeight: '1.5' }]`
- Images: use Vite `import` for processed assets or `public/` for static serving. Always set `aspect-ratio` or explicit `width`/`height` to prevent CLS. Hero images: `fetchpriority="high"` + `loading="eager"`. Below-fold: `loading="lazy"`.
- Data-dense components: switch to accordion or swipeable cards on mobile — never horizontal scroll tables

### Step 5 — Code Patterns

Provide concrete Tailwind + React code for the recommended patterns.

**Color strategy based on Step 2 detection:**
- **shadcn/ui detected** (`components.json` or `@radix-ui/*`): use semantic tokens — `bg-background`, `text-foreground`, `text-muted-foreground`, `border-border`
- **Vanilla Tailwind** (no shadcn): use standard palette — `bg-white dark:bg-slate-950`, `text-slate-900 dark:text-slate-50`, `text-slate-500 dark:text-slate-400`, `border-slate-200 dark:border-slate-800`

**Focus ring standard** (all code examples use this):
- On light backgrounds: `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-offset-2 focus-visible:ring-blue-600`
- On dark backgrounds: add `focus-visible:ring-offset-slate-900 dark:focus-visible:ring-offset-white`
- With shadcn/ui: `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-offset-2 focus-visible:ring-ring`

**Animation strategy based on Step 2 detection:**
- `framer-motion` installed → use motion components
- `tailwindcss-animate` installed → use `animate-in` classes
- Neither → use custom keyframes or CSS transitions

**File structure recommendation** (for greenfield projects):
```
components/
  ui/          ← atoms: Button, Input, Badge, Card
  sections/    ← composites: Hero, Nav, FeatureGrid, CTA
  layouts/     ← page shells: MarketingLayout, AppLayout
lib/
  utils.ts     ← cn() helper if using class merging
```

**Reference — Sticky Nav with transparent-on-scroll (vanilla Tailwind):**

```tsx
import { useState, useEffect, useRef } from "react";

export function StickyNav() {
  const [scrolled, setScrolled] = useState(false);
  const scrolledRef = useRef(false);

  useEffect(() => {
    const onScroll = () => {
      const isScrolled = window.scrollY > 20;
      if (isScrolled !== scrolledRef.current) {
        scrolledRef.current = isScrolled;
        setScrolled(isScrolled);
      }
    };
    window.addEventListener("scroll", onScroll, { passive: true });
    return () => window.removeEventListener("scroll", onScroll);
  }, []);

  return (
    <>
      {/* Skip link — requires <main id="main"> in your page layout */}
      <a
        href="#main"
        className="sr-only focus:not-sr-only focus:absolute focus:z-50 focus:bg-white focus:text-slate-900 dark:focus:bg-slate-900 dark:focus:text-white focus:px-4 focus:py-2"
      >
        Skip to content
      </a>
      <header
        className={`fixed inset-x-0 top-0 z-40 transition-all duration-300 ${
          scrolled
            ? "bg-white/90 shadow-sm backdrop-blur-md dark:bg-slate-950/90"
            : "bg-transparent"
        }`}
      >
        <nav className="container mx-auto flex items-center justify-between px-4 py-4">
          <a href="/" className="text-xl font-bold text-slate-900 dark:text-white">
            {/* TODO: Replace with your brand name or logo */}
            Brand
          </a>
          <div className="hidden items-center gap-6 md:flex">
            <a href="#features" className="text-sm text-slate-600 hover:text-slate-900 dark:text-slate-300 dark:hover:text-white">
              Features
            </a>
            <a href="#pricing" className="text-sm text-slate-600 hover:text-slate-900 dark:text-slate-300 dark:hover:text-white">
              Pricing
            </a>
            <button className="rounded-lg bg-slate-900 px-4 py-2 text-sm font-medium text-white hover:bg-slate-800 focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-offset-2 focus-visible:ring-blue-600 dark:bg-white dark:text-slate-900 dark:hover:bg-slate-100">
              Get Started
            </button>
          </div>
        </nav>
      </header>
    </>
  );
}
```

interface FeatureCardProps {
  title: string;
  description: string;
  icon: React.ReactNode;
}

export function FeatureCard({ title, description, icon }: FeatureCardProps) {
  return (
    <div className="group rounded-2xl border border-slate-200 bg-white p-6 transition-all duration-200 hover:-translate-y-1 hover:shadow-lg dark:border-slate-800 dark:bg-slate-900 dark:hover:border-blue-500/30">
      <div className="mb-4 flex h-10 w-10 items-center justify-center rounded-lg bg-blue-50 text-blue-600 dark:bg-blue-950 dark:text-blue-400">
        {icon}
      </div>
      <h3 className="text-lg font-semibold text-slate-900 dark:text-white">{title}</h3>
      <p className="mt-2 text-sm leading-6 text-slate-600 dark:text-slate-400">{description}</p>
    </div>
  );
}

**Reference — Feature card (shadcn/ui variant):**

```tsx
import { Card, CardContent, CardHeader, CardTitle } from "@/components/ui/card";

interface FeatureCardProps {
  title: string;
  description: string;
  icon: React.ReactNode;
}

export function FeatureCard({ title, description, icon }: FeatureCardProps) {
  return (
    <Card className="group transition-all duration-200 hover:-translate-y-1 hover:shadow-lg dark:hover:border-blue-500/30">
      <CardHeader>
        <div className="mb-2 flex h-10 w-10 items-center justify-center rounded-lg bg-primary/10 text-primary">
          {icon}
        </div>
        <CardTitle>{title}</CardTitle>
      </CardHeader>
      <CardContent>
        <p className="text-sm text-muted-foreground">{description}</p>
      </CardContent>
    </Card>
  );
}
```
